TSLA: Total revenues for 2025 were $94.83 billion, down $2.86 billion year-over-year, with net income attributable to common stockholders of $3.79 billion, a decrease of $3.30 billion from 2024. Produced 1.66 million and delivered 1.64 million consumer vehicles in 2025; launched Robotaxi service in June 2025. Ended 2025 with $44.06 billion in cash, cash equivalents, and investments, up $7.50 billion from 2024. Automotive sales revenue fell 9% to $65.82 billion; automotive regulatory credits revenue dropped 28% to $1.99 billion. Energy generation and storage revenue rose 27% to $12.77 billion. R&D expenses increased 41% to $6.41 billion, mainly due to AI and product roadmap expansion. Capital expenditures expected to exceed $20 billion in 2026. Ongoing challenges include trade policy uncertainty, inflation, rising interest rates, and evolving government incentives. Exposure to supply chain disruptions, inflation, and tariffs impacting costs and production. Restructuring actions in 2025 led to $390 million in charges related to AI chip design convergence.
PLTR: Q4 2025 U.S. commercial revenue surged 137% year-over-year and 28% quarter-over-quarter, reaching $507 million. Total Q4 2025 revenue grew 70% year-over-year and 19% quarter-over-quarter to $1.41 billion. FY 2025 revenue increased 56% year-over-year to $4.48 billion, with U.S. commercial revenue up 109% year-over-year. Record total contract value of $4.26 billion in Q4, up 138% year-over-year. Q4 2025 GAAP net income was $609 million (43% margin). FY 2025 adjusted income from operations was $2.25 billion (50% margin). Q1 2026 revenue expected between $1.532-$1.536 billion. FY 2026 revenue guidance of $7.182-$7.198 billion, representing 61% year-over-year growth. Contracts often include termination for convenience provisions, impacting revenue realization. Rapid advancements in AI models driving operational leverage and growth.
MSFT: Revenue reached $81.3 billion for the quarter ended December 31, 2025, up 17% year-over-year. Operating income was $38.3 billion, up 21% year-over-year. Net income on a GAAP basis was $38.5 billion, up 60% year-over-year; non-GAAP net income was $30.9 billion, up 23%. Microsoft Cloud revenue surpassed $50 billion, increasing 26% year-over-year. Intelligent Cloud revenue was $32.9 billion, up 29%; Azure and other cloud services up 39%. More Personal Computing revenue was $14.3 billion, down 3%. Commercial remaining performance obligation increased 110% to $625 billion. Non-GAAP results exclude a $7.6 billion net gain from investments in OpenAI. Risks include intense competition, execution risks in cloud and AI, large investments, cybersecurity threats, and regulatory and legal challenges. $12.7 billion returned to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases.
META: Revenue grew 24% year-over-year in Q4 2025 and 22% for the full year, reaching $59.89B and $200.97B respectively. Net income for Q4 2025 was $22.77B, up 9% year-over-year; full-year net income was $60.46B, down 3% from 2024. Full-year diluted EPS was $23.49, impacted by One Big Beautiful Bill Act which significantly increased the effective tax rate in Q3 2025. Reality Labs Q4 2025 operating loss was $6.02 billion; full-year RL operating loss was $19.19 billion. Long-term debt increased to $58.74B at year-end, up from $28.83B in 2024. 2026 capital expenditures expected to be $115B-$135B. Q1 2026 revenue expected between $53.5B-$56.5B. Ongoing legal and regulatory headwinds in the EU and U.S. could materially impact results. Continued scrutiny on youth-related issues and scheduled U.S. trials may result in material losses.
